purpose: Worker agent lifecycle and responsibilities. Defines the fixed stage sequence (reading_context -> planning -> implementing -> testing -> finalizing -> completed/failed), the two critical paths ({tracker_root} vs {project_root}), the eight non-negotiable rules every worker follows, and the structured return format (STATUS, SUMMARY, FILES CHANGED, EXPORTS, ANNOTATIONS, DIVERGENT ACTIONS, WARNINGS, ERRORS).
gotchas: Each worker is the SOLE writer of its progress file -- no other agent reads or modifies it
Partial completion (>=80% done with a blocker) uses status:'completed' with a clear summary, NOT 'failed'
Workers must always include dashboard_id in every progress write or the server rejects it
patterns: Self-contained dispatch prompts so workers need no master callbacks
conventions: Progress writes use the Write tool (atomic temp-rename), never echo/cat shell redirects
Live timestamps via date -u +'%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ' -- never reconstructed from memory
